design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot a combat helicopter through mission-based objectives using machine gun, rockets, and guided missiles while managing altitude, heading, and weapon resources
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing flight simulation complexity with arcade accessibility; players navigate between 'real heli mode' (harder, more freedom) and standard controls (easier, more constrained)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a military attack helicopter in tactical combat scenarios with authentic flight mechanics and weapon systems
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Steel Talons commits to helicopter simulation authenticity (analog collective lever for altitude, rudder pedals) while explicitly offering accessibility through toggleable difficulty ('real heli mode'), signaling that complexity should not exclude players. The arcade cabinet design with cockpit theming doubles down on immersion and embodied controls rather than abstraction.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can toggle 'real heli mode' to increase challenge and flight freedom, suggesting intentional difficulty scaling. Standard mode prioritizes playability; real heli mode rewards mastery with tactical advantages.

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon loadouts include machine gun, rockets, and limited air-to-surface guided missiles managed per-mission
progression.unlock_mechanism: 19 missions in single-player/cooperative modes; competitive mode removes mission structure in favor of helicopter-vs-helicopter dueling
replayability_hooks: Cooperative and competitive multiplayer modes offer different progression philosophies
Real heli mode toggles entire flight physics for mastery-focused replays
19-mission campaign encouragable for speedrunning or high-score pursuit

world_building_techniques: Mission-based scenario framing (implied military/combat context through helicopter loadouts and target types)
Arcade cabinet theming with cockpit visuals establishes military aesthetic

faction_indicators: Military/NATO helicopter (AT1196 designation suggests Western defense contractor origin)
Cooperative vs. competitive mode split reflects alliance vs. adversarial social structures
danger_signals: Air-to-surface guided missiles indicate power projection and ranged superiority
Helicopter platform suggests rapid mobility and strike capability
Multiplayer deathmatch framing normalizes aerial combat escalation
environmental_storytelling: Cockpit arcade cabinet design emphasizes occupant control and isolation
Flat-shaded polygon 3D rendering creates abstracted, dehumanized battlefield visualization
Weapon progression (machine gun → rockets → guided missiles) mirrors real military doctrine escalation
